title: melaleuca aestuosa
intro: Melaleuca aestuosa, also known as the Swamp Paperbark or Swamp Tea Tree, is a species of plant in the myrtle family, native to Australia. The medicinal properties of Melaleuca aestuosa include its essential oil, which contains compounds like cineole and limonene, and is known for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Melaleuca aestuosa include its potential to help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in wound care and skin conditions like acne and eczema. Herbal preparations of Melaleuca aestuosa include tea, essential oil, and infused oil, which can be used topically or taken internally, depending on the specific preparation and intended use.
